name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
description: A central government scheme providing income support of ₹6,000 per year to farmer families across the country, paid in three equal installments of ₹2,000 every four months.
eligibility: Small and marginal farmer families
Combined landholding of up to 2 hectares
Must have cultivable land in their name
Indian citizen

name: Ration Card (PDS)
description: A government-issued document that allows eligible families to purchase subsidized food grains and other essential commodities from fair price shops under the Public Distribution System.
eligibility: Resident of the state where applying
Annual family income below state-defined threshold
No existing ration card in the family
BPL/APL/AAY category as applicable
documents: Proof of residence (electricity bill / rent agreement)
ministry: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution

name: Ayushman Bharat (PM-JAY)
description: World's largest government-funded health insurance scheme providing coverage of ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization to over 10 crore poor and vulnerable families.
eligibility: Families listed in SECC 2011 database
Deprived rural families (D1–D7 categories)
Urban workers in defined occupational categories
No upper age limit
documents: Ration Card or SECC data proof
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana
description: Provides free LPG connections to women from Below Poverty Line households to replace unclean cooking fuels and improve health outcomes, especially for rural women.
eligibility: Woman aged 18 years or above
BPL family as per SECC 2011
No existing LPG connection in household
Resident of rural or urban area
documents: BPL card or SECC list proof
ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
benefit: Free LPG connection + first refill subsidy

name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana
description: National mission for financial inclusion to ensure access to financial services such as banking, savings, credit, insurance, and pension for all households at affordable costs.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen aged 10 years and above
No existing bank account required
Zero minimum balance requirement
Even minors above 10 can open accounts
documents: Voter ID / PAN Card / Driving License (alternative)
benefit: Zero-balance account + ₹2 lakh accident insurance + overdraft facility

name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana
description: Provides loans up to ₹10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises for income-generating activities in manufacturing, trading, and services.
eligibility: Indian citizen above 18 years
Non-farm small or micro business
No defaulter of any bank/financial institution
Valid business plan or existing business
